# UC-13.3.2 · Prometheus Metrics Ingestion Health (OpenTelemetry Collector Prometheus Receiver scrape failures, target-up gaps, federation lag, remote-write back-pressure, sample-rate burn through Splunk Metrics)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Availability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the plumbing that moves numeric measurements from your applications into the central store so breaks in collection, delays in forwarding, or sudden floods of labels show up before charts go blank or costs spike without a clear reason.*

---

## Description

Detects end-to-end Prometheus and OpenTelemetry metric ingest failures into Splunk Metrics indexes by correlating target `up` scrape health, federation and scrape-duration signals, collector exporter errors from logs and zPages, Kubernetes ServiceMonitor or PodMonitor reconciliation events, Splunk tcpin-style receiver pressure, and cardinality burn markers, enriched with inventory ownership metadata.

## Value

Platform and observability leaders shorten mean time to innocence when dashboards look quiet yet the metric path is compromised: scrape outages, federation staleness, collector drops, or receiver throttling starve trustworthy series before business KPIs move. Executives receive an evidence-backed ledger that ties third-party metric telemetry health to ownership tiers so licensing risk, reliability incidents, and change windows stay explainable.

## Implementation

Land `sourcetype=prometheus:up`, `sourcetype=prometheus:metrics`, `sourcetype=otel:collector:log`, `sourcetype=otel:collector:zpages`, and `sourcetype=kube:events` into observability indexes, supplement `_internal` metrics receiver logs when licensed, publish `prometheus_target_inventory.csv`, normalize cluster labels, save `prometheus_otel_metric_ingest_health_uc_13_3_2` on a fifteen-minute cadence with earliest four hours, route page and warn severities through the platform on-call channel, and archive weekly evidence exports for governance.

## Evidence

Saved search `prometheus_otel_metric_ingest_health_uc_13_3_2` on a fifteen-minute schedule; observability dashboard drilldowns across `prometheus:up`, `prometheus:metrics`, OTel sourcetypes, Kubernetes events, and `_internal` metrics logs; alert routing to platform observability on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab cluster, induce a sustained scrape failure on a non-production target while `prometheus:up` reports zero for three consecutive minutes, forward matching `otel:collector:log` exporter errors, and confirm `prometheus_otel_metric_ingest_health_uc_13_3_2` returns `page` or `warn` with non-null `recommended_action` within fifteen minutes.

### Negative scenario

After recovery when Prometheus Targets show healthy scrapes, federation samples are fresh, collector logs quiet, and `_internal` tcpin logs show no throttle signatures, confirm severities return to `info` and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to `page` and `warn` only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability engineers who operate Prometheus servers, meta-Prometheus federation tiers, OpenTelemetry Collector fleets, Kubernetes monitoring operators, and the teams that administer the metrics indexing tier on Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud. This use case treats Prometheus exposition and the OpenTelemetry Collector as the customer metric source of truth for platform health, then proves whether the end-to-end path into Splunk Metrics indexes remains faithful: scrapes succeed, federation hops stay fresh, collector receivers and exporters stay error-free, remote write or HTTP Event Collector bridges do not throttle silently, and cardinality does not burn license or performance budgets. It is intentionally not a catalog of business KPI dashboards built from application golden signals; it is telemetry about telemetry, focused on the third-party metric ingest chain your organization depends on before any downstream dashboard can be trusted.

Prometheus publishes counters, gauges, histograms, and summaries using text exposition formats documented by the Prometheus project. The scrape contract assumes targets expose `/metrics` or an equivalent scrape path, respond within configured timeouts, honor labels consistently, and cooperate with relabeling rules that decide which targets enter which scrape jobs. The synthetic gauge family `up` encodes scrape health per `job` and `instance` labels: a value of one means the last scrape succeeded and parsed, while zero means the scrape failed, timed out, or could not be decoded. Interpreting `up` requires remembering that intentional outages, canary removals, and maintenance windows can drive zeros without implying Splunk ingestion failure; correlation with change metadata and inventory lookups prevents reflexive paging. Federation extends this model: a meta-Prometheus may scrape `/federate` endpoints from leaf Prometheus instances, aggregate selected series, and expose them upstream. Federation lag appears as stale timestamps, growing evaluation delays, or widening gaps between leaf `last scrape` times and what the meta tier materializes. TLS expiry on federation peers, DNS re-resolution churn in service meshes, and partial rule evaluation storms can break the chain even when individual exporters answer `/metrics` quickly.

The OpenTelemetry Collector can ingest Prometheus exposition through a dedicated receiver that scrapes targets or accepts remote write style payloads depending on configuration, then routes points through processors such as batching, memory limiting, attribute filtering, and routing connectors before exporters ship to Splunk through OTLP, Signalfx, Prometheus remote write adapters, or vendor-specific Splunk Distribution paths. Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector packaging on Splunkbase, plus Splunk Connect for Kubernetes, are common deployment vehicles for cluster-local collectors and agents. Understanding the difference between a Prometheus remote write exporter sending native remote-write frames and a Collector Prometheus receiver performing fresh scrapes matters when you triage duplication, lag, or cardinality doubling during migrations. Splunk Metrics indexes impose licensing and indexer CPU constraints that tighten as label cardinality grows; high-cardinality labels such as unbounded `user_id`, `pod_ip`, or per-request identifiers can explode time-series counts observable through `prometheus_tsdb_head_series` style signals when those lines are forwarded into Splunk.

RBAC and access hygiene are non-negotiable. Read-only automation accounts should retrieve `/api/v1/targets`, `/api/v1/status/config`, `/metrics`, and OpenTelemetry zPages or diagnostics endpoints using scoped tokens or kubeconfig identities tied to observability namespaces. Break-glass cluster-admin or static kubeadmin-equivalent bearer tokens must never land in saved searches, macros, or long-lived repository copies. Network policy must allow scrape paths from collector subnets to application namespaces without flattening segmentation entirely; document approved paths per zone. Legal and privacy review should confirm which labels may be stored in Splunk, especially when application teams embed customer identifiers; relabel `drop` actions may be required before indexing.

Risk briefing: single-target scrape flaps during rolling restarts routinely toggle `up` for one or two intervals. This use case therefore enforces a three-event dwell using `streamstats` minima across the `prom_up` lane before page severity triggers, while still surfacing warn-tier hints for slower burns. Federation lag thresholds must track your hierarchical scrape intervals; a meta tier that intentionally scrapes every few minutes should not page on sub-minute skew. Queue-oriented use cases such as UC-13.3.10 remain the home for deep exporter queue saturation analytics; UC-13.3.5 remains focused on collector restart cadence and process churn counts. This document complements them by tying leaf scrape truth, federation freshness, collector error logs, zPages exporter failures, Kubernetes operator reconciliation noise, and Splunk metrics receiver pressure into one operational narrative.

Differentiation recap: end-to-end Prometheus and OpenTelemetry metric path integrity into Splunk Metrics indexes, not generic golden-signal KPI dashboards, not pure collector restart counting alone, not exclusive queue-depth analysis without scrape context, not security detection of malicious metric poisoning unless you add sibling controls.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes the multisearch arm fans wide, plus a CSV inventory that supplies ownership context without hard-coding team names into SPL.

Lane A, target-up truth: ingest Prometheus scrape snapshots with `sourcetype=prometheus:up` into a metrics-friendly index such as `prometheus_metrics`, `metrics`, or `observability_metrics`. Preserve `job`, `instance`, `cluster`, and any `replica` labels your exporters emit. If you rely on recording rules that recreate `up`, ensure they do not mask scrape failures by constant folding. Forwarders should stamp `k8s_cluster_name` or `openshift_cluster` consistently so multisearch arms coalesce cleanly.

Lane B, exposition and federation analytics: ingest canonical Prometheus text lines or pre-parsed metric rows with `sourcetype=prometheus:metrics`. Include series such as `scrape_duration_seconds`, `prometheus_target_sync_length_seconds`, `prometheus_rule_group_duration_seconds`, `prometheus_remote_storage_*` when remote write is native to Prometheus, and `prometheus_tsdb_head_series` for cardinality pressure. For meta-Prometheus tiers, ensure federation scrape jobs label which leaf cluster they represent so lag math can group by `cluster` and `job`.

Lane C, collector error narratives: ship OpenTelemetry Collector logs with `sourcetype=otel:collector:log` from DaemonSets, sidecars, or gateway deployments. Enable structured logging or JSON where possible so exporter names, endpoints, and retry counts parse reliably. Tune `FILE` or `splunk_hec` exporters to avoid duplicating the same error at high volume without rate limiting on the forwarder.

Lane D, introspection and exporter health: optionally scrape Collector self-telemetry or zPages-derived snapshots into `sourcetype=otel:collector:zpages` or an equivalent diagnostics sourcetype. Capture counters that reflect send failures, enqueue refusals, or gRPC status errors on exporters targeting Splunk. Keep scrape intervals modest to avoid self-reflective metric storms.

Lane E, Kubernetes control-plane context: forward `sourcetype=kube:events` from clusters running Prometheus Operator style ServiceMonitor and PodMonitor resources. Include events that mention reconciliation errors, mount failures for webhooks, or CrashLoopBackOff on `prometheus` or `collector` pods. Supplement with Splunk internal `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunkd` files such as `metrics.log` or tcpin-related sources that reveal receiver-side throttling when metrics pipelines apply back-pressure.

Inventory lookup: maintain `prometheus_target_inventory.csv` with columns including `target_endpoint`, `job_instance`, `cluster_tier`, `owner_team`, and `environment`. The `target_endpoint` column should carry the normalized URL or host:port key used during joins; `job_instance` should carry `job/instance` style keys when that is how operators think about ownership. Refresh the lookup weekly from configuration management or service catalog exports.

Example curl helpers for design reviews, executed from bastion hosts with proper credentials:

```bash
curl -fsS http://prometheus:9090/api/v1/targets | jq '.data.activeTargets[] | {job: .labels.job, health: .health}'
curl -fsS http://prometheus:9090/metrics | head
curl -fsS http://otel-collector:55679/debug/tracez | head
```

props.conf guidance: set `TRUNCATE` high enough for wide Prometheus lines, use `LINE_BREAKER` appropriate to multiplexed syslog framing if present, and apply `FIELDALIAS` or `EVAL` to normalize `__name__` versus `metric_name` depending on TA version. For Kubernetes events, ensure `involvedObject_name` and `reason` extract cleanly.

Pre-save validation: run short-window searches confirming each sourcetype produces rows in the last hour during normal operations, that `prometheus:up` includes both ones and zeros occasionally in lab chaos tests, and that OTel logs show benign startup lines before you tune error filters.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as `prometheus_otel_metric_ingest_health_uc_13_3_2` with a fifteen-minute schedule in steady-state production, `dispatch.earliest_time` aligned to at least four hours for federation context, and `dispatch.latest_time` set to now. Alert when `severity` is `page` or `warn`, throttle duplicate `cluster` and `entity_key` pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates, and include `recommended_action`, `coalesce_lane`, and `cond_msg` fragments in pager descriptions so incident commanders open the right runbook appendices without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: `multisearch` fans the `prom_up`, `prom_metrics`, `otel_log`, `otel_zpages`, and combined Kubernetes plus Splunk internal arm so a silent outage in one sourcetype does not hide systemic failure elsewhere. Within the `prom_up` arm, one-minute bucketing plus `streamstats` over fifteen steps approximates a rolling fifteen-minute up ratio per target key. After arms unify, another `streamstats` pass enforces three consecutive degraded minutes on the `prom_up` lane before `dwell_bad` marks a page-worthy scrape outage, reducing noise from single-interval flaps. `eventstats` lifts cluster-level sample burn peaks to contextualize cardinality spikes. `case()` ladders encode severity and recommended actions. The closing `join` enriches rows with `cluster_tier`, `owner_team`, and `environment` from `prometheus_target_inventory.csv` matched on `inv_match` derived from `target_endpoint` or `job_instance` equivalents.

Fenced SPL must match the `spl` JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.2 Prometheus and OpenTelemetry metric ingest path health — tunables earliest=-4h@h streamstats_window=15m bucket=1m dwell_windows=3")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel) sourcetype=prometheus:up earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster, openshift_cluster, prometheus_cluster, "unknown"))))
      | eval job=trim(toString(coalesce(job, prometheus_job, "")))
      | eval instance=trim(toString(coalesce(instance, scrape_instance, "")))
      | eval inv_match=lower(trim(coalesce(instance, strcat(job,"/",instance), strcat(job,":",instance), "")))
      | eval entity_key=strcat(job,":",instance)
      | eval upv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, up_metric, Value, "1")),10)
      | eval lane="prom_up"
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats latest(upv) AS upv BY cluster entity_key inv_match _time
      | sort 0 + cluster + entity_key + inv_match + _time
      | streamstats window=15 global=f current=t avg(upv) AS up_ratio_15m BY cluster entity_key inv_match
      | eval federation_lag_min=null()
      | eval otel_drop_rate=null()
      | eval smc_remote_write_lag=null()
      | eval sample_burn_score=null()
      | eval deg_f=if(upv==0 OR isnull(upv),1,0)
      | eval cond_msg=if(deg_f==1,"target_up_zero_or_null","target_up_ok")
      | fields _time cluster lane entity_key inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score deg_f cond_msg ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster, openshift_cluster, "unknown"))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, "0")),10)
      | where match(mn,"prometheus_tsdb_head_series|prometheus_rule_group_duration_seconds|scrape_duration_seconds|prometheus_remote_storage|prometheus_target_sync_length_seconds|^up$")
      | eval job=trim(toString(coalesce(job, "")))
      | eval instance=trim(toString(coalesce(instance, "")))
      | eval inv_match=lower(trim(coalesce(instance, strcat(job,"/",instance), "")))
      | eval entity_key=strcat(mn,":",coalesce(instance,"agg"))
      | eval lane="prom_metrics"
      | eval up_ratio_15m=null()
      | eval federation_lag_min=if(match(mn,"target_sync|rule_group"), if(mv>1.8, mv*60, mv), null())
      | eval otel_drop_rate=null()
      | eval smc_remote_write_lag=if(match(mn,"remote_storage|remote_write|shard|queue"), mv, null())
      | eval sample_burn_score=if(match(mn,"prometheus_tsdb_head_series"), mv, null())
      | eval deg_f=case(match(mn,"scrape_duration") AND mv>25, 1, match(mn,"prometheus_tsdb_head_series") AND mv>7500000, 1, true(), 0)
      | eval cond_msg=strcat(mn,"=",tostring(round(mv,4)))
      | fields _time cluster lane entity_key inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score deg_f cond_msg ]
    [ search (index=otel OR index=containers OR index=observability_logs) sourcetype=otel:collector:log earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster, "unknown"))))
      | eval rawl=lower(_raw)
      | where match(rawl,"exporter error|consumer dropped|send failed|retry|backpressure|throttl|refused|deadline exceeded")
      | eval inv_match=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(target, instance, pod, "otel_collector"))))
      | eval entity_key=strcat("otel_log_",coalesce(pod,host,"unknown"))
      | eval lane="otel_log"
      | eval up_ratio_15m=null()
      | eval federation_lag_min=null()
      | eval otel_drop_rate=1
      | eval smc_remote_write_lag=null()
      | eval sample_burn_score=null()
      | eval deg_f=1
      | eval cond_msg=substr(_raw,1,320)
      | fields _time cluster lane entity_key inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score deg_f cond_msg ]
    [ search (index=otel OR index=containers OR index=observability_metrics) sourcetype=otel:collector:zpages earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, k8s_cluster_name, "unknown"))))
      | eval inv_match=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_instance_id, instance, "otel_zpages"))))
      | eval entity_key="otel_zpages_probe"
      | eval lane="otel_zpages"
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, name, __name__, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, "0")),10)
      | eval up_ratio_15m=null()
      | eval federation_lag_min=null()
      | eval otel_drop_rate=if(match(mn,"send_failed|dropped|refused|enqueue"), mv, null())
      | eval smc_remote_write_lag=null()
      | eval sample_burn_score=null()
      | eval deg_f=if(mv>0 AND match(mn,"failed|dropped|refused"),1,0)
      | eval cond_msg=strcat(coalesce(mn,"zpages"),"=",tostring(round(mv,4)))
      | fields _time cluster lane entity_key inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score deg_f cond_msg ]
    [ search (((index=kubernetes OR index=kube OR index=openshift) sourcetype=kube:events (_raw=*ServiceMonitor* OR _raw=*PodMonitor* OR _raw=*prometheus* OR _raw=*collector* OR reason=*BackOff* OR reason=*Failed*)) OR (index=_internal (source=*metrics.log OR source=*tcpin*) sourcetype=splunkd))
        earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, k8s_cluster_name, "unknown"))))
      | eval inv_match=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_name, object_name, host, servername, "k8s_or_internal"))))
      | eval entity_key=case(sourcetype=="kube:events", strcat("evt_",inv_match), true(), strcat("splunk_int_",coalesce(host,"idx")))
      | eval lane=if(sourcetype=="kube:events","kube_evt","splunk_metrics_int")
      | eval up_ratio_15m=null()
      | eval federation_lag_min=null()
      | eval otel_drop_rate=null()
      | eval smc_remote_write_lag=if(sourcetype=="splunkd" AND match(lower(_raw),"tcpin|queue|blocked|throttl|refused|reset"), 55, null())
      | eval sample_burn_score=null()
      | eval deg_f=case(sourcetype=="kube:events" AND match(lower(_raw),"error|failed|backoff|unhealthy|oom"),1, sourcetype=="splunkd" AND match(lower(_raw),"refused|reset|close|throttle|limit|blocked"),1, true(),0)
      | eval cond_msg=substr(_raw,1,260)
      | fields _time cluster lane entity_key inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score deg_f cond_msg ]
| sort 0 + cluster + entity_key + lane + _time
| streamstats window=3 global=f min(deg_f) AS min3deg BY cluster entity_key lane
| eval dwell_bad=if(lane=="prom_up" AND min3deg==1 AND deg_f==1,1,0)
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(up_ratio_15m) AS up_ratio_15m max(federation_lag_min) AS federation_lag_min max(otel_drop_rate) AS otel_drop_rate max(smc_remote_write_lag) AS smc_remote_write_lag max(sample_burn_score) AS sample_burn_score max(deg_f) AS deg_f max(dwell_bad) AS dwell_bad values(lane) AS lanes first(cond_msg) AS cond_msg BY cluster entity_key inv_match
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lanes),0)
| eventstats max(sample_burn_score) AS cluster_sample_peak BY cluster
| eval severity=case(
    dwell_bad==1 AND match(coalesce_lane,"prom_up"), "page",
    deg_f==1 AND match(coalesce_lane,"otel_log|otel_zpages"), "page",
    coalesce(federation_lag_min,0)>180, "page",
    coalesce(federation_lag_min,0)>90, "warn",
    coalesce(smc_remote_write_lag,0)>60, "page",
    coalesce(smc_remote_write_lag,0)>30, "warn",
    coalesce(sample_burn_score,0)>9000000, "page",
    coalesce(sample_burn_score,0)>5500000, "warn",
    match(coalesce_lane,"kube_evt") AND deg_f==1, "warn",
    match(coalesce_lane,"splunk_metrics_int") AND deg_f==1, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    dwell_bad==1, "inspect_prometheus_target_state_exporter_tls_and_scrape_timeout_then_validate_otel_prometheus_receiver",
    match(coalesce_lane,"otel_log|otel_zpages"), "tail_collector_logs_check_exporter_queues_memory_limits_and_batch_sizes",
    coalesce(smc_remote_write_lag,0)>30, "check_splunk_metrics_tcpin_backpressure_remote_write_endpoint_capacity_license_headroom",
    coalesce(sample_burn_score,0)>5500000, "attack_label_cardinality_review_metric_relabel_allowlists_drop_rules_and_aggregation",
    match(coalesce_lane,"kube_evt"), "correlate_servicemonitor_podmonitor_rollouts_with_scrape_failures",
    true(), "trace_federation_hops_tls_sni_and_meta_prometheus_match_rules")
| join type=left max=0 inv_match
    [| inputlookup prometheus_target_inventory.csv
     | eval inv_match=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(target_endpoint, job_instance, ""))))
     | fields inv_match cluster_tier owner_team environment]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier, "unrated"), owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, "unowned"), environment=coalesce(environment, "unknown")
| table cluster entity_key coalesce_lane inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score dwell_bad severity recommended_action last_seen cond_msg cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[prometheus_otel_metric_ingest_health_uc_13_3_2_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-observability-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Metric path $result.severity$ $result.cluster$ $result.entity_key$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch prometheus_otel_metric_ingest_health_uc_13_3_2 | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal service level objectives, summarize per-minute `prom_up` and high-cardinality `prometheus:metrics` extracts into hourly summary indexes keyed on `cluster`, `entity_key`, and `inv_match`, widen alert searches to `earliest=-2h@h` on the summary for detection, and retain this full search for investigations. For very large Prometheus fleets, push aggressive relabel `keep` rules upstream to shrink `prom_metrics` volume before it reaches Splunk.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the Prometheus UI or equivalent: open the Targets page, filter to failing jobs, and compare scrape error messages to `cond_msg` snippets in Splunk for the same `job` and `instance` within one collection interval. When statuses disagree, verify clock skew between Prometheus, collectors, and indexers, duplicate `cluster` labels across regions, and stale forwarder buffers.

Deep dive with `curl` against `/api/v1/targets` for active and dropped targets, capturing `lastError` strings. For federation chains, compare timestamps on leaf `up` series against what the meta tier serves on `/federate` queries for identical selectors. TLS errors should match federation scrape logs and OTel exporter logs when collectors proxy traffic.

OpenTelemetry validation: tail collector logs on representative pods, confirm exporter errors align with `otel:collector:log` rows, and hit zPages or internal self-metrics endpoints to verify counters move when you inject a controlled exporter failure in non-production. Recovery drills should show severities returning to `info`.

Kubernetes validation: `kubectl describe` ServiceMonitor or PodMonitor objects that changed recently, and confirm `kube:events` rows in Splunk mirror warning events you see live. For Splunk internal arms, reproduce a benign metrics throughput test in lab and confirm `metrics.log` lines reference tcpin health without crossing warn thresholds on healthy indexers.

Synthetic negative test: after recovery when targets show `up=1`, federation queries return fresh samples, collectors show quiet logs, and internal metrics receivers look idle, confirm the alert wrapper returns zero rows for `page` and `warn` filters.

Runbook linkage: document expected federation delays during cross-region failovers so validators do not file defects when leaf clusters pause intentionally.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Prometheus target down for thirty minutes with three-interval dwell: confirm exporter process health, TLS trust, network policy, and scrape timeout budgets; validate Service discovery records; then inspect OTel Prometheus receiver scrape configs for mismatched labels.

Case 2 — Scrape timeout while manual `curl` of `/metrics` succeeds: investigate `scrape_duration_seconds` quantiles, concurrent scrape limits, and reverse-proxy buffering; tune `scrape_timeout` and Collector `timeout` settings coherently so leaf and collector agree.

Case 3 — Federation chain breaks due to TLS expiry on a meta hop: renew certificates, verify SNI hostnames, reload federation scrape jobs, and document leaf-to-meta label mapping so selectors still match after rotation.

Case 4 — OTel Collector out-of-memory kills dropping points: raise memory limits under vendor guidance, reduce batch sizes, add memory limiter processor thresholds, and ensure attribute filtering trims high-cardinality labels before export.

Case 5 — OTel exporter queue full backing pressure into receivers: increase queue capacity cautiously, validate downstream Splunk endpoint concurrency, and ensure UC-13.3.10 queue analytics align with this search before muting.

Case 6 — Splunk Metrics index hot-bucket roll causing brief receive lag: correlate with indexer maintenance, verify remote write clients retry with backoff, and distinguish transient lag from sustained `smc_remote_write_lag` elevation.

Case 7 — Label-cardinality explosion blowing through TSDB head-series limits: execute relabel `drop` rules, enforce allowlists, aggregate recording rules upstream, and engage service owners when application metrics embed unbounded identifiers.

Case 8 — ServiceMonitor `labelSelector` mismatch preventing scrapes: fix selector symmetry between Services and monitors, reconcile Helm chart upgrades, and confirm Prometheus Operator logs show successful sync.

Case 9 — OTel agent rolling restart in Kubernetes producing benign error bursts: treat as non-pageable when dwell thresholds clear within minutes, annotate change metadata, and suppress only with verified rollout windows.

Case 10 — HTTP Event Collector token rotation gap causing exporter auth errors: rotate tokens with overlap, update Collector secrets, and verify logs transition from 401 storms to clean exports before closing incidents.

Case 11 — False calm when `props.conf` reroutes sourcetype names: confirm transforms still map to expected `prometheus:up` and `prometheus:metrics` labels; stale routing hides lanes and silences this detection.

Case 12 — Dual-collector race duplicating samples and masking `up` semantics: enforce single scraper ownership per target, dedupe via relabel hashes, and document architecture decisions to prevent competing Prometheus receivers.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.2 Prometheus and OpenTelemetry metric ingest path health — tunables earliest=-4h@h streamstats_window=15m bucket=1m dwell_windows=3")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel) sourcetype=prometheus:up earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster, openshift_cluster, prometheus_cluster, "unknown"))))
      | eval job=trim(toString(coalesce(job, prometheus_job, "")))
      | eval instance=trim(toString(coalesce(instance, scrape_instance, "")))
      | eval inv_match=lower(trim(coalesce(instance, strcat(job,"/",instance), strcat(job,":",instance), "")))
      | eval entity_key=strcat(job,":",instance)
      | eval upv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, up_metric, Value, "1")),10)
      | eval lane="prom_up"
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats latest(upv) AS upv BY cluster entity_key inv_match _time
      | sort 0 + cluster + entity_key + inv_match + _time
      | streamstats window=15 global=f current=t avg(upv) AS up_ratio_15m BY cluster entity_key inv_match
      | eval federation_lag_min=null()
      | eval otel_drop_rate=null()
      | eval smc_remote_write_lag=null()
      | eval sample_burn_score=null()
      | eval deg_f=if(upv==0 OR isnull(upv),1,0)
      | eval cond_msg=if(deg_f==1,"target_up_zero_or_null","target_up_ok")
      | fields _time cluster lane entity_key inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score deg_f cond_msg ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster, openshift_cluster, "unknown"))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, "0")),10)
      | where match(mn,"prometheus_tsdb_head_series|prometheus_rule_group_duration_seconds|scrape_duration_seconds|prometheus_remote_storage|prometheus_target_sync_length_seconds|^up$")
      | eval job=trim(toString(coalesce(job, "")))
      | eval instance=trim(toString(coalesce(instance, "")))
      | eval inv_match=lower(trim(coalesce(instance, strcat(job,"/",instance), "")))
      | eval entity_key=strcat(mn,":",coalesce(instance,"agg"))
      | eval lane="prom_metrics"
      | eval up_ratio_15m=null()
      | eval federation_lag_min=if(match(mn,"target_sync|rule_group"), if(mv>1.8, mv*60, mv), null())
      | eval otel_drop_rate=null()
      | eval smc_remote_write_lag=if(match(mn,"remote_storage|remote_write|shard|queue"), mv, null())
      | eval sample_burn_score=if(match(mn,"prometheus_tsdb_head_series"), mv, null())
      | eval deg_f=case(match(mn,"scrape_duration") AND mv>25, 1, match(mn,"prometheus_tsdb_head_series") AND mv>7500000, 1, true(), 0)
      | eval cond_msg=strcat(mn,"=",tostring(round(mv,4)))
      | fields _time cluster lane entity_key inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score deg_f cond_msg ]
    [ search (index=otel OR index=containers OR index=observability_logs) sourcetype=otel:collector:log earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster, "unknown"))))
      | eval rawl=lower(_raw)
      | where match(rawl,"exporter error|consumer dropped|send failed|retry|backpressure|throttl|refused|deadline exceeded")
      | eval inv_match=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(target, instance, pod, "otel_collector"))))
      | eval entity_key=strcat("otel_log_",coalesce(pod,host,"unknown"))
      | eval lane="otel_log"
      | eval up_ratio_15m=null()
      | eval federation_lag_min=null()
      | eval otel_drop_rate=1
      | eval smc_remote_write_lag=null()
      | eval sample_burn_score=null()
      | eval deg_f=1
      | eval cond_msg=substr(_raw,1,320)
      | fields _time cluster lane entity_key inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score deg_f cond_msg ]
    [ search (index=otel OR index=containers OR index=observability_metrics) sourcetype=otel:collector:zpages earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, k8s_cluster_name, "unknown"))))
      | eval inv_match=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_instance_id, instance, "otel_zpages"))))
      | eval entity_key="otel_zpages_probe"
      | eval lane="otel_zpages"
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, name, __name__, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, "0")),10)
      | eval up_ratio_15m=null()
      | eval federation_lag_min=null()
      | eval otel_drop_rate=if(match(mn,"send_failed|dropped|refused|enqueue"), mv, null())
      | eval smc_remote_write_lag=null()
      | eval sample_burn_score=null()
      | eval deg_f=if(mv>0 AND match(mn,"failed|dropped|refused"),1,0)
      | eval cond_msg=strcat(coalesce(mn,"zpages"),"=",tostring(round(mv,4)))
      | fields _time cluster lane entity_key inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score deg_f cond_msg ]
    [ search (((index=kubernetes OR index=kube OR index=openshift) sourcetype=kube:events (_raw=*ServiceMonitor* OR _raw=*PodMonitor* OR _raw=*prometheus* OR _raw=*collector* OR reason=*BackOff* OR reason=*Failed*)) OR (index=_internal (source=*metrics.log OR source=*tcpin*) sourcetype=splunkd))
        earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, k8s_cluster_name, "unknown"))))
      | eval inv_match=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_name, object_name, host, servername, "k8s_or_internal"))))
      | eval entity_key=case(sourcetype=="kube:events", strcat("evt_",inv_match), true(), strcat("splunk_int_",coalesce(host,"idx")))
      | eval lane=if(sourcetype=="kube:events","kube_evt","splunk_metrics_int")
      | eval up_ratio_15m=null()
      | eval federation_lag_min=null()
      | eval otel_drop_rate=null()
      | eval smc_remote_write_lag=if(sourcetype=="splunkd" AND match(lower(_raw),"tcpin|queue|blocked|throttl|refused|reset"), 55, null())
      | eval sample_burn_score=null()
      | eval deg_f=case(sourcetype=="kube:events" AND match(lower(_raw),"error|failed|backoff|unhealthy|oom"),1, sourcetype=="splunkd" AND match(lower(_raw),"refused|reset|close|throttle|limit|blocked"),1, true(),0)
      | eval cond_msg=substr(_raw,1,260)
      | fields _time cluster lane entity_key inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score deg_f cond_msg ]
| sort 0 + cluster + entity_key + lane + _time
| streamstats window=3 global=f min(deg_f) AS min3deg BY cluster entity_key lane
| eval dwell_bad=if(lane=="prom_up" AND min3deg==1 AND deg_f==1,1,0)
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(up_ratio_15m) AS up_ratio_15m max(federation_lag_min) AS federation_lag_min max(otel_drop_rate) AS otel_drop_rate max(smc_remote_write_lag) AS smc_remote_write_lag max(sample_burn_score) AS sample_burn_score max(deg_f) AS deg_f max(dwell_bad) AS dwell_bad values(lane) AS lanes first(cond_msg) AS cond_msg BY cluster entity_key inv_match
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lanes),0)
| eventstats max(sample_burn_score) AS cluster_sample_peak BY cluster
| eval severity=case(
    dwell_bad==1 AND match(coalesce_lane,"prom_up"), "page",
    deg_f==1 AND match(coalesce_lane,"otel_log|otel_zpages"), "page",
    coalesce(federation_lag_min,0)>180, "page",
    coalesce(federation_lag_min,0)>90, "warn",
    coalesce(smc_remote_write_lag,0)>60, "page",
    coalesce(smc_remote_write_lag,0)>30, "warn",
    coalesce(sample_burn_score,0)>9000000, "page",
    coalesce(sample_burn_score,0)>5500000, "warn",
    match(coalesce_lane,"kube_evt") AND deg_f==1, "warn",
    match(coalesce_lane,"splunk_metrics_int") AND deg_f==1, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    dwell_bad==1, "inspect_prometheus_target_state_exporter_tls_and_scrape_timeout_then_validate_otel_prometheus_receiver",
    match(coalesce_lane,"otel_log|otel_zpages"), "tail_collector_logs_check_exporter_queues_memory_limits_and_batch_sizes",
    coalesce(smc_remote_write_lag,0)>30, "check_splunk_metrics_tcpin_backpressure_remote_write_endpoint_capacity_license_headroom",
    coalesce(sample_burn_score,0)>5500000, "attack_label_cardinality_review_metric_relabel_allowlists_drop_rules_and_aggregation",
    match(coalesce_lane,"kube_evt"), "correlate_servicemonitor_podmonitor_rollouts_with_scrape_failures",
    true(), "trace_federation_hops_tls_sni_and_meta_prometheus_match_rules")
| join type=left max=0 inv_match
    [| inputlookup prometheus_target_inventory.csv
     | eval inv_match=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(target_endpoint, job_instance, ""))))
     | fields inv_match cluster_tier owner_team environment]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier, "unrated"), owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, "unowned"), environment=coalesce(environment, "unknown")
| table cluster entity_key coalesce_lane inv_match up_ratio_15m federation_lag_min otel_drop_rate smc_remote_write_lag sample_burn_score dwell_bad severity recommended_action last_seen cond_msg cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance host=* earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS metric_host
| join type=left max=0 metric_host
    [| inputlookup prometheus_target_inventory.csv
     | rename target_endpoint AS metric_host
     | fields metric_host cluster_tier owner_team environment]
| where like(metric_host,"%prom%") OR like(metric_host,"%otel%") OR like(metric_host,"%collector%")
| table metric_host cpu_load mem_used cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by cluster and entity_key with drilldowns to raw `prometheus:up` rows, `prometheus:metrics` samples, OTel logs, zPages extracts, Kubernetes events, `_internal` tcpin lines, and lookup ownership columns; timeline of `up_ratio_15m` and `federation_lag_min` for executive summaries.

## Known False Positives

Scheduled relabel or `push` style jobs that intentionally remove targets during autoscaling can drive `up` gaps that clear within one interval; pair with change metadata before paging. Exporter rolling restarts and canary pod churn produce short `up` flaps that the three-interval dwell should absorb, yet very aggressive scrape intervals can still false-negative dwell logic if only two failures occur. Splunk Metrics index hot-bucket rolls and indexer restart waves create brief tcpin lag signatures in `_internal` logs that resemble back-pressure even when remote-write clients are healthy; correlate across peers before declaring receiver saturation. Federation mesh DNS re-resolution after service mesh upgrades can stall meta scrapes until caches warm; treat warn-tier lag as informational when leaf clusters report fresh timestamps. Kubernetes ServiceMonitor or PodMonitor churn during GitOps apply storms emits noisy events that reconcile cleanly; require message keywords tied to failures, not merely object updates. OpenTelemetry Collector pipeline restarts emit exporter retry lines that self-resolve; cross-check zPages counters for sustained growth. HTTP Event Collector token rotation without dual-write overlap can create auth error bursts that look like sustained ingest failure until secrets converge. Downstream PagerDuty or third-party observability backend maintenance windows may pause alert delivery even while Splunk rows show warn severities; route proofs through a secondary channel during vendor maintenance. Dual Prometheus receivers scraping the same targets can duplicate samples and confuse cardinality math until architecture owners dedupe scrape ownership.
